body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: I have observed several disappointing aspects of teaching quality in my Journalism courses at the School of Communication this semester that I feel compelled to bring to attention. Certain lectures are delivered without adequate structure, making it difficult to follow the logical progression of content, and some lecturers rely heavily on reading directly from slides rather than elaborating or encouraging discussion. Assessment criteria are sometimes communicated after assignments are already underway, which creates unnecessary confusion and unfair evaluation conditions. I strongly raise concerns about the institution to invest in faculty development programs focused on modern pedagogy, student engagement techniques, and constructive feedback practices to address these shortcomings urgently.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The Journalism curriculum at the School of Communication covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are satisfactory in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: Assessment practices in my Journalism program at the School of Communication are generally acceptable, though there is meaningful room for improvement in both consistency and transparency. Some modules employ well-designed assessments that genuinely test comprehension and application, while others rely too heavily on high-stakes end-of-semester examinations that do not adequately reflect students' ongoing learning throughout the term. Timely feedback is provided by some lecturers but is inconsistent across the department. Standardizing minimum feedback turnaround times and encouraging a more diverse portfolio of assessment types would significantly improve the learning experience for all students.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer accessibility has been a problematic aspect of my experience in the Journalism program at the School of Communication this semester, and I believe it warrants serious attention. Office hours are infrequently held and rarely communicated through official channels, making it difficult for students to plan consultations around their own timetables. Email responses are often delayed by more than a week, and some lecturers appear unresponsive to student queries outside of formal class time entirely. This lack of availability creates unnecessary barriers to learning and discourages students from seeking help when they need it most. I urge the school to enforce minimum availability standards for all faculty members.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: Learning resources for my Journalism program at the School of Communication are adequate, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is impressive and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Communication this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I praise the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: The academic administrative processes managed by the Academic Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been excellent this semester, and I value the efficiency and transparency with which they are handled. Course registration was smooth and intuitive via the student portal, with clear instructions provided well in advance of enrollment deadlines. Grade appeals and transcript requests are processed promptly and communicated through official channels with regular status updates that reduce student anxiety. Administrative staff are approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful when students encounter difficulties navigating university policies or procedures. The overall administrative experience reflects a student-centered approach that I believe enhances the broader university experience significantly.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been exceptional and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also praise the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: The physical campus environment and facilities managed by the Facilities Management provide a impressive learning atmosphere that I believe genuinely contributes to academic productivity and student well-being at the School of Communication. Lecture halls are spacious, well-ventilated, and equipped with modern audio-visual technology that facilitates clear and engaging presentations. Study spaces — including individual carrels, group collaboration rooms, and outdoor relaxation areas — are thoughtfully designed and well-maintained throughout the academic week. Cleaning and security standards are consistently high, and campus maintenance requests are typically resolved within a reasonable timeframe. The overall physical environment reflects a commitment to providing students with a safe, comfortable, and inspiring place to learn.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: My key recommendation for the School of Communication is to urgently address the concerning systemic issues that have affected Journalism students this semester, rather than treating them as isolated incidents. A structured, transparent student feedback mechanism — where concerns are acknowledged within a set timeframe and outcomes communicated back to students — would significantly rebuild trust that has been eroded by unaddressed issues. I also strongly recommend leadership to conduct a comprehensive curriculum and service quality review in genuine consultation with current students, alumni, and industry partners. Without meaningful action on the concerns raised in this feedback, student satisfaction and the institution's competitive standing will continue to decline in the years ahead.
